userStories.title: Fix CommentThread SSR/hydration and stale data
userStories.description: Remove `await` from the `useAsyncData` call in CommentThread.vue to eliminate the SSR hydration mismatch. Replace the one-time `ref` assignment with a `computed` so comments stay reactive after refresh. Replace `comments.value.push(newComment)` with a `refreshComments()` call after a successful post.
acceptanceCriteria: When CommentThread mounts on the server, the useAsyncData call does NOT use the await keyword
When data.value changes (e.g., after refresh), the comments template renders the updated list because comments is a computed(() => data.value ?? [])
When onSubmit successfully posts a new comment, refreshComments() is called instead of pushing to comments.value
When onSubmit successfully posts a new comment, resetForm() is still called after refreshComments()
When useAsyncData is pending, the template renders the loading text via the existing v-if="pending" branch
When useAsyncData returns an error, the template renders the error text via the existing v-else-if="error" branch

userStories.title: Add loading and error states to all pages
userStories.description: Destructure `pending` and `error` from every `useAsyncData` call across the six affected pages. Wrap existing page content in a v-else block and add v-if="pending" (loading) and v-else-if="error" (error + retry button) sibling blocks. Add the missing i18n keys `common.loadFailed` and `common.retry` to en.json and zh.json.
acceptanceCriteria: When pages/index.vue useAsyncData is pending, the template renders a centered 'Loading...' element and hides the project grid
When pages/index.vue useAsyncData resolves with an error, the template renders t('common.loadFailed') and a Button that calls refresh()
When pages/[project]/index.vue useAsyncData is pending, the template renders a loading state and hides the ticket board
When pages/[project]/index.vue useAsyncData resolves with an error, the template renders an error message and a retry Button
When pages/[project]/agents.vue useAsyncData is pending, the template renders a loading state
When pages/[project]/agents.vue useAsyncData resolves with an error, the template renders an error message and a retry Button that calls refresh()
When pages/[project]/labels.vue useAsyncData resolves with an error, the template renders an error message and a retry Button
When pages/[project]/tickets/[ref].vue useAsyncData is pending, the template renders a loading state via v-if="pending" (not the hardcoded 'Loading ticket...' text shown unconditionally)
When pages/[project]/tickets/[ref].vue useAsyncData resolves with an error, the template renders an error message and a retry Button
When pages/[project]/kb.vue useAsyncData is pending, the template renders a loading state
When pages/[project]/kb.vue useAsyncData resolves with an error, the template renders an error message and a retry Button
Given en.json is loaded, t('common.loadFailed') returns 'Failed to load data'
Given en.json is loaded, t('common.retry') returns 'Retry'
Given zh.json is loaded, t('common.loadFailed') returns '加载失败'
Given zh.json is loaded, t('common.retry') returns '重试'

userStories.title: Fix register page layout and error handling
userStories.description: Remove the redundant full-screen wrapper div from register.vue (the auth layout already provides it). Replace the inline `error` ref and banner div with `toast.error(extractApiError(err))` on failure and `toast.success(t('toast.loggedIn'))` on success, matching the login page pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: When register.vue is rendered, the outermost template element is <div class="w-full max-w-md space-y-8"> with no outer flex min-h-screen wrapper
When handleRegister throws an error, toast.error(extractApiError(err)) is called instead of setting an error ref
When handleRegister succeeds, toast.success(t('toast.loggedIn')) is called before navigateTo('/')
When register.vue script is loaded, toast is imported from 'vue-sonner'
When register.vue script is loaded, there is no `error` ref variable declared
When register.vue template is rendered, there is no v-if="error" banner div in the template
priorErrors: Attempt 1 failed with model tier: fast: Autofix exhausted: review still failing after fix attempts

priorFailures.summary: Failed with tier fast, escalating to next tier

reviewFindings.message: Diff contains only test files — the implementation file pages/register.vue is not included. All 6 acceptance criteria target register.vue behaviour but there is no evidence that the file was modified to satisfy them.

AC1 template extraction uses a non-greedy regex /<template>([\s\S]*?)<\/template>/ which will match only up to the first </template> closing tag. Vue SFCs routinely contain <template> slots inside components, causing the regex to capture a truncated subtree and produce a false pass.

The toast-before-navigateTo ordering test is wrapped in a double conditional (if tryBlockMatch … if toastIndex !== -1 && navigateIndex !== -1). If the try-block regex fails to match, or if either string is absent, the test passes vacuously with no assertion executed.

AC5 second test checks for error.value = but not for patterns like error.value = null or error.value = '' used to clear the ref after a successful call. A ref could exist and be used for non-assignment reads and still slip past this check.

userStories.title: Make logout async and remove duplicate sidebar UI
userStories.description: In useAuth.ts, make `logout()` async and await `navigateTo('/login')` so callers can chain on the promise. In layouts/default.vue, remove the sidebar bottom section (lines 57-68) containing the duplicate user email and logout button — the header already has these controls.
acceptanceCriteria: When useAuth().logout() is called, it returns a Promise<void>
When useAuth().logout() is called, it sets token.value to null and user.value to null before awaiting navigateTo('/login')
When useAuth().logout() resolves, navigateTo('/login') has been awaited (not fire-and-forget)
When default.vue sidebar is rendered, there is no <div class="border-t border-border p-4"> section containing user email and logout button
When default.vue sidebar is rendered, the nav links section (NuxtLink elements) is still present and unchanged
When default.vue header is rendered, the user email span and logout button are still present in the header

userStories.title: Use internal API URL for SSR requests
userStories.description: In useApi.ts and useAuth.ts, compute baseURL using `import.meta.server ? useRuntimeConfig().apiInternalUrl : useRuntimeConfig().public.apiBaseUrl` so that SSR requests bypass the Nuxt proxy and call the API service directly. Both composables need the same check independently — useAuth.ts uses $fetch directly to avoid circular dependency with useApi.
acceptanceCriteria: When useApi() runs on the server (import.meta.server is true), baseURL equals useRuntimeConfig().apiInternalUrl
When useApi() runs on the client (import.meta.server is false), baseURL equals useRuntimeConfig().public.apiBaseUrl
When useAuth() runs on the server (import.meta.server is true), baseURL equals useRuntimeConfig().apiInternalUrl
When useAuth() runs on the client (import.meta.server is false), baseURL equals useRuntimeConfig().public.apiBaseUrl
When useAuth.ts makes an auth/login request during SSR, the request URL is constructed as `${useRuntimeConfig().apiInternalUrl}/auth/login`
When useApi.ts makes a GET request during SSR, the request URL is constructed as `${useRuntimeConfig().apiInternalUrl}${path}`

analysis:
All 5 stories target apps/web only — no API or CLI changes needed.

**US-001 (CommentThread):** apps/web/components/CommentThread.vue line 31 uses `await useAsyncData(...)` — this causes Nuxt SSR hydration mismatch because the parent [ref].vue page does not wrap the component in <Suspense>. Lines 36-37 assign the fetched data to a plain `ref` once, so the comments list goes stale after a manual refresh. Line 67 mutates `comments.value.push(newComment)` — once we move to `computed`, this will break at runtime. Fix: remove `await`, change `ref` to `computed(() => data.value ?? [])`, and call `refreshComments()` after a successful post. The `pending`/`error` destructuring already exists and is used in the template (lines 80-81) — they must remain.

**US-002 (Loading/error states):** apps/web/pages/index.vue line 70 does not destructure `pending` or `error` from `useAsyncData`. All six affected pages follow the same pattern: they call `useAsyncData` but only use the returned data, leaving users on an infinite blank screen on network error. The fix is purely additive — wrap existing content in a `v-else` block and add `v-if="pending"` and `v-else-if="error"` sibling blocks. i18n key `common.loading` already exists in en.json/zh.json (line 6). Keys `common.loadFailed` and `common.retry` are missing and must be added to both locale files. The Button component from shadcn-nuxt is already available everywhere. Risk: none — the happy path is unchanged.

**US-003 (Register page):** apps/web/pages/register.vue has `definePageMeta({ layout: 'auth' })` (line 80) but the template at line 2 wraps everything in `<div class="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center bg-background">` — this duplicates the wrapper already provided by apps/web/layouts/auth.vue. The `error` ref (line 87) and inline error banner (lines 11-13) must be replaced with `toast.error(extractApiError(err))` matching the login page pattern. `toast` from `vue-sonner` is not currently imported in register.vue. `extractApiError` is already imported (line 78). Reference: apps/web/pages/login.vue uses `toast.error()` and `toast.success()` patterns.

**US-004 (Cleanup):** apps/web/composables/useAuth.ts line 58-62 — `logout()` is a sync function that calls `navigateTo('/login')` without `await`, meaning callers cannot know when navigation completes. apps/web/layouts/default.vue lines 57-68 — sidebar bottom section duplicates the user email and logout button already in the header (lines 92-100). Removing the sidebar section only; nav links (lines 20-53) must remain untouched.

**US-005 (SSR API URL):** apps/web/composables/useApi.ts line 93 sets `baseURL = config.public.apiBaseUrl` (always `/api`). During SSR, `/api` is a relative path that hits the Nuxt proxy, which then forwards to the real API — an unnecessary extra round-trip that also breaks in non-Docker environments without the proxy configured. apps/web/composables/useAuth.ts line 31 has the same issue. apps/web/nuxt.config.ts line 79 already configures `runtimeConfig.apiInternalUrl` (server-side) and `runtimeConfig.public.apiBaseUrl` (client-side). The fix is a one-liner in each composable: `const baseURL = import.meta.server ? useRuntimeConfig().apiInternalUrl : useRuntimeConfig().public.apiBaseUrl`. Note: useAuth.ts uses `$fetch` directly (not useApi) to avoid circular dependency — the comment at line 24-28 must be preserved. TypeScript: `useRuntimeConfig()` returns `{ apiInternalUrl: string, public: { apiBaseUrl: string } }` so no type casting needed.
